result.text: Duke basketball vs Siena prediction in NCAA Tournament first round Skip to main content Home NFL MLB NBA NHL NCAA Football NCAA Basketball UFC/MMA PGA Tour Games WNBA Newsletter Soccer NASCAR CFL Bet 101 Industry News Betting Map Online Casino USA TODAY Sports Duke basketball vs Siena prediction: Our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Anna Snyder Fayetteville Observer Duke basketball opens the 2026 Men's NCAA Tournament against Siena at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, South Carolina. The top-seeded Blue Devils (32-2) and head coach Jon Scheyer will face No. 16 seed Siena (23-11) and head coach Gerry McNamara on Thursday, March 19 at 2:50 p.m. ET on ESPN . It marks the second-straight season Duke is a No. 1 seed in March Madness and the 16th time in program history. The Saints are the Metro Atlantic Athletic Conference champions and earned their first NCAA Tournament berth since 2010. A win over Siena advances Duke to face the winner between No. 8 seed Ohio State and No. 9 seed TCU on Saturday night. Other teams in the East Region include UConn, Michigan State and Louisville. Duke won the ACC regular season and tournament titles for the second-straight season. They are coming off back-to-back league tournament titles for the first time since the 2010 and 2011 seasons as Jon Scheyer is the first ACC coach to win three tournament titles in his first four seasons. Here's a scouting report and prediction for Duke vs. Siena. Buy Duke basketball tickets for the 2026 NCAA Tournament Isaiah Evans, Patrick Ngongba II, Maliq Brown, Darren Harris have NCAA Tournament experience While bringing in five freshmen, Duke also returns March Madness experience from the 2025 tournament in senior Maliq Brown and sophomores Isaiah Evans, Patrick Ngongba II and Darren Harris. Brown, who went out during last year's ACC Tournament, returned for the NCAA Tournament with a 2-for-2 mark with three assists and two steals in 15 minutes against Houston. Harris played 11 minutes against Mount St. Mary's, scoring seven points, and three minutes against Baylor. Evans averaged 7.6 minutes per game in last year's tournament, with three points against Arizona and two against Alabama. He posted a career-high 32 points with seven triples in Duke's win over Florida State to open the 2026 ACC Tournament, adding a career-high 10 rebounds the next night vs. Clemson. He finished with 20 against Virginia after being held without a field goal for a majority of the first half. He is Duke's second-leading scorer with 14.9 points per game. Ngongba played in all five of Duke's NCAA Tournament games last season, with a tournament-high nine points in the Round of 64 win over Mount St. Mary's and eight each in the wins over Baylor and Alabama. He did not play in the ACC Tournament but is hopeful for a return for March Madness. Scheyer said after the win against the Cavaliers that it was a "day-by-day thing" but that he feels "very positive about it." Ngongba is Duke's third-leading sco

pick.rationale: Duke should dominate this 1 vs 16 matchup given the massive talent and efficiency gap between the teams. The Blue Devils rank #1 nationally in both Torvik and KenPom with elite offense (128.0 AdjO) and the nation's best defense (90.4 AdjD), while Siena ranks around 190th nationally. Despite some injury concerns with Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba earlier in the ACC Tournament, Duke still has Cooper Flagg, Kon Knueppel, and Isaiah Evans leading a deep roster that won both the ACC regular season and tournament titles. Siena's inspiring MAAC Tournament championship run is commendable, but the Saints lack the talent, size, and efficiency to compete with a historically dominant Duke team in the Round of 64.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs Siena Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke enters as the #1 overall seed with a 31-2 record and top rankings in all major metrics, facing MAAC champion Siena (23-11) in a classic 1 vs 16 first-round matchup. The Blue Devils possess overwhelming advantages in talent, efficiency, and depth.
evidence: Duke ranks #1 in both Torvik and KenPom with 128.0 AdjO (4th) and 90.4 AdjD (1st), creating a 41-point efficiency margin advantage
Siena ranks 187-192 nationally with significantly lower efficiency metrics across both offense and defense
Duke won the ACC regular season and tournament despite late-season injuries, demonstrating depth with stars like Cooper Flagg, Kon Knueppel, and Isaiah Evans
Siena earned their first NCAA bid since 2010 by winning the MAAC Tournament as a 3-seed but faces a historically difficult task as a 16-seed
